title: Depravity of the American (and Phoenix) Press: A Defense of Jeff Durbin
author: James Silberman
orientation: Silberman defends Apologia Church pastor Jeff Durbin against a Phoenix New Times piece by Katya Schwenk that insinuated a connection between Durbin and QAnon because a single Q administrator had cited a Durbin sermon. Drawing on William Lloyd Garrison's 1858 essay 'Depravity of the American Press,' Silberman argues the modern mainstream press functions as a slanderous protector of the abortion industry by manufacturing guilt-by-association hit pieces against abolitionists.
audiences: Pastors and church leaders weighing public engagement
History-minded readers interested in Garrison-era parallels

questions.question: What is the central charge Silberman levels against the American press in this piece?
questions.answer: He characterizes the American press as a powerful institution serving the abortion industry by trafficking in deception and slander, willing to distort or invent stories to advance its political ends rather than to report truthfully.
questions.quote: The serial killers of the abortion industry make up the most depraved institution in American society, followed by a number of powerful institutions which serve their interests. This would include — among many others — Hollywood, teacher’s unions, and the American press; which is, by and large, a collection of vicious, scheming calumniators who have proven themselves willing to murder the truth in pursuance of their political objectives.

questions.question: What was the Phoenix New Times article actually claiming about Jeff Durbin?
questions.answer: Schwenk's article tried to manufacture a connection between Durbin and the QAnon movement because a single QAnon administrator had cited one of Durbin's sermons as motivation to run for office, even though Durbin himself rejects QAnon.
questions.quote: The *Phoenix New Times* published an [article](https://www.phoenixnewtimes.com/news/qanon-ringleader-ron-watkins-congress-jeff-durbin-mesa-arizona-12249888) on Friday attempting to connect Jeff Durbin of Apologia Church and End Abortion Now to the conspiracy theorist movement because a QAnon administrator credits one of Durbin’s sermons as his inspiration to run for office.

questions.question: According to Silberman, what two questions should have settled the story before it ran?
questions.answer: Whether Durbin himself supports QAnon, and whether the QAnon administrator in question is a member of Apologia Church. Both answers were no, which Silberman argues should have killed the piece outright.
questions.quote: Does Durbin support QAnon? Is the man a member of Apologia Church? These are the only questions that mattered, but they did not to the *Phoenix New Times*. Both of these questions being answered resoundingly in the negative did not discourage them from running the story.

questions.question: How does Silberman distinguish a legitimate story from the hit piece Schwenk published?
questions.answer: He argues that a pastor actively spreading QAnon would be newsworthy, but the inverse — a single QAnon figure listening to a pastor who repudiates Q out of hundreds of thousands of listeners — is not.
questions.quote: A pastor propagating Q to hundreds of thousands of YouTube listeners is a story. A Q administrator being one of hundreds of thousands of listeners to a pastor who utterly repudiates Q is not.

questions.question: Why does Silberman say the deception will compound even though Schwenk technically noted Durbin's rejection of QAnon?
questions.answer: Because downstream outlets will pick up the framing without the buried caveat. He cites Matthew Chapman at Raw Story already having repeated the smear without noting Durbin's repudiation of Q.
questions.quote: Matthew Chapman at [*Raw Story*](https://www.rawstory.com/qanon-leader-motivated-to-run-for-congress-by-far-right-pastor-who-advocates-for-abortions-report/) picked up Schwenk’s coverage and repeated much of her reporting without noting that Durbin rejects QAnon. Because of Schwenk and Chapman’s deception, there will be leftists running around believing Durbin is connected to Q.

questions.question: What political motive does Silberman assign to this kind of media slander?
questions.answer: He identifies the underlying objective as protecting the abortion industry — what he calls the literal child sacrifice happening across America — by discrediting its loudest opponents.
questions.quote: the mainstream media’s political objectives include protecting child sacrifice (the real poisoning, suffocating, and tearing apart of live children limb-from-limb all across America, [not the imagined child sacrifice of Q](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2OFbC1miIyI)).

questions.question: How does William Lloyd Garrison's 1858 essay frame the press of his own day, and why is it cited here?
questions.audience: History-minded readers interested in Garrison-era parallels
questions.answer: Garrison described the press as cowardly, mercenary, lie-coining men who exploit a position of public trust to mislead readers and protect entrenched evils. Silberman invokes him to argue that the modern press behaves identically toward abolitionists of abortion.
questions.quote: “The American press is, to a fearful extent, in the hands of a cowardly, mercenary and unprincipled class of men, who have no regard for truth in dealing with what is unpopular; who cater to the lowest passions of the multitude, and caricature every movement aiming at the overthrow of established wrong; who are as destitute of all fairness in controversy as they are lacking in self-respect; and whose columns are closed against any reply that may be proffered to their libellous accusations.

questions.question: What parallel does Garrison draw between press treatment of abolitionism and other unpopular reform movements?
questions.audience: History-minded readers interested in Garrison-era parallels
questions.answer: He observes that for a quarter century anti-slavery meetings could not be held without being misrepresented or ignored, and that every other noble unpopular movement received the same treatment from the journals of the day.
questions.quote: for a quarter of a century, abolitionism—the denial of the right to make man the property of man—has been lampooned, anathematized, vilified, unceasingly and universally, by the journals of the day, both religious and secular—its advocates have been held up as crazy fanatics and wild disorganizers—and its meetings represented as unworthy of countenance by sane and decent men! Every other unpopular movement, however noble and good, has been treated in the same manner.

questions.question: What practical action does Silberman urge readers to take in response to slanderous coverage?
questions.audience: Pastors and church leaders weighing public engagement
questions.answer: He calls readers to disengage from journalists like Schwenk and Chapman, subscribe directly to Apologia Studios and Free the States on YouTube, and continue pressing the abolitionist cause despite mounting slander.
questions.quote: Reduce your reliance on “journalists” like Katya Schwenk and Matthew Chapman. Subscribe to [Apologia Studios](https://www.youtube.com/c/ApologiaStudios) and [Free the States](https://www.youtube.com/c/FreetheStates) on YouTube, and press on in the abolitionist cause despite the slander which will get worse and worse as the day of justice for preborn children gets closer and closer.

related.title: Refuting Dana Hall McCain's Nonsensical Anti-Abolitionist Slander
related.reason: Another Silberman-era response to a media smear of abolitionists, showing the same pattern of guilt-by-association attacks the Durbin piece dissects.

related.title: Greg Treat Calls Out FTS and Slanders Us on the Senate Floor
related.reason: A parallel case study in how powerful actors slander abolitionists publicly, reinforcing the article's broader thesis about hostile institutional gatekeepers.

related.title: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
related.reason: Demonstrates how mainstream media framing is weaponized against abolition legislation, mirroring the QAnon-smear tactic critiqued here.

related.title: SBC President Slanders SBC Pastor
related.reason: Shows the same slander dynamic playing out inside the church, complementing the Durbin defense's focus on guarding pastors from manufactured accusations.

related.title: Why Do Pro-Lifers Despise Abolitionists?
related.reason: Explains the broader hostility abolitionists face from sympathetic-seeming institutions, contextualizing why the press chooses targets like Durbin.

related.title: Immediate, Not Gradual Abolition of Abortion
related.reason: Grounds the Garrison-flavored framing of this piece in the immediatist abolitionist tradition that Silberman is invoking and defending.

related.title: Never Be Impressed By a Politician's Claim to Be "100% Pro-Life"
related.reason: Another Garrison-tagged Free the States essay critiquing the political and rhetorical machinery that protects the abortion industry from accountability.
